title: Itchy Skin Relief: 11 Herbal Tinctures to Try

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for itchy skin because it helps reduce inflammation and soothe irritation, making it easier to calm and heal the skin.
Kaempferol is good for itchy skin because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and soothe irritation, providing relief from itchy skin conditions.
Tannins are good for itchy skin because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and discomfort.
Flavonoids are good for itchy skin because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and soothe the skin, providing relief from itching and discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis tincture is good for itchy skin because it has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. This plant has been used for centuries to treat skin problems. The tincture is made from the flowers of the plant and contains compounds that calm and reduce swelling. When applied to itchy skin, it helps to ease discomfort and promote healing. It also has antimicrobial properties that prevent infections and promote a healthy skin environment. By reducing inflammation and killing bacteria, the tincture helps to soothe itchy skin and promote a smooth, calm skin surface. The tincture can be applied directly to the affected area, and it is often used in combination with other treatments to help manage itchy skin conditions.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Plantago lanceolata herbal tincture that helps with itchy skin, start by gathering fresh or dried leaves of the plant. If using fresh leaves, dry them in a low-temperature oven or by air-drying them for 2-3 weeks. Once dried, add 1 part of the dried leaves to 2 parts of a solvent like vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar. The ratio is important, as it helps to extract the active compounds from the plant. Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the mixture infuse. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. Your tincture is now ready to use. To apply it to itchy skin, simply add a few drops to a carrier oil like coconut oil or olive oil and gently massage it onto the affected area.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica tincture is made from the leaves of the stinging nettle plant. This plant is known for its itchy and irritating effect on skin when touched. However, when made into a tincture and applied topically, it can have the opposite effect. The tannins and other compounds in the plant help to reduce inflammation and itching in itchy skin. Urtica dioica tincture may be helpful for people with eczema, hives, and other skin conditions that cause itchy skin. The tincture can be applied directly to the affected area, or added to a bath for a soothing and calming effect. It's also thought to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce redness and swelling in the skin. When used correctly, Urtica dioica tincture may provide relief from itchy skin and promote healthy skin healing.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Plantago lanceolata herbal tincture to help with itchy skin, first, you'll need to gather the herb, which can be found in most health food stores or grown in your own garden. Harvest the leaves and flowers of the Plantago lanceolata plant and dry them thoroughly in a warm, dark place or using a food dehydrator. Once the herb is dry, place it in a clean glass jar with a 1:5 ratio of dried herb to vodka or a high-proof spirit, such as 80-proof vodka. Fill the jar completely with the liquid, making sure the herb is fully submerged. Close the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every few days. After the steeping time, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids. Store the tincture in a cool, dark place and use 20-30 drops of the tincture in water or taken sublingually as needed to help soothe itchy skin.
remedies.image_alt: Urtica dioica herbal tinctures for itchy skin

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tincture is a liquid extract from the aloe vera plant. It's good for itchy skin because it has anti-inflammatory properties. When you apply it to itchy skin, it helps reduce swelling and redness. Aloe vera also has soothing and calming effects, which can ease the discomfort of itchy skin. It has natural cooling properties that can bring down the heat and irritation of itchy skin. The tincture is also rich in vitamins and minerals like vitamin E and vitamin C, which can help nourish and moisturize the skin. This can help heal itchy skin faster and prevent future irritation. Additionally, aloe vera has antiseptic properties, which can help prevent infection and promote healing.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Plantago lanceolata herbal tincture for itchy skin, you'll need some fresh or dried plant leaves. Start by gathering a handful of the leaves, then chop or bruise them to release their oils and flavors. Next, combine the Plantago lanceolata leaves with a neutral liquid solvent, such as high-proof vodka or glycerin, in a clean glass jar. The general ratio is one part plant material to five parts solvent, so for example, you would use 1 cup of chopped leaves with 5 cups of vodka. Store the jar in a cool, dark place and let it sit for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally. This process, called maceration, allows the plant's active compounds to infuse into the solvent. After the infusion time, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean jar, discarding the solids. Your Plantago lanceolata tincture is now ready to use, typically by taking a few drops of it sublingually (under the tongue) or adding it to a carrier oil for topical application.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum tincture, also known as St John's Wort, is a natural remedy for itchy skin. It has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and redness in the skin. The tannins in the tincture have a soothing effect, which calms the skin and reduces irritation. When applied topically, the active compounds in Hypericum perforatum tincture can ease the discomfort and itching caused by minor skin irritations, bug bites, and even sunburn. The tincture also has antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent infection in broken skin. By reducing inflammation and soothing the skin, Hypericum perforatum tincture can provide quick relief from itchy skin.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Plantago lanceolata herbal tincture that helps with itchy skin, you'll need a few simple ingredients and some basic equipment. First, gather a handful of fresh Plantago lanceolata leaves or use dried ones, depending on what's available. Clean the leaves thoroughly and chop them into small pieces to release their medicinal properties. Next, combine the chopped leaves with a high-proof vodka or ethanol in a clean glass jar, making sure the leaves are completely covered by the liquid. The general ratio is one part plant material to five parts liquid. Close the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place, allowing the mixture to steep for two to four weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the liquid penetrate the leaves. After the steeping process, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Your Plantago lanceolata tincture is now ready to use - simply add a few drops to your skin or take it internally, following the recommended dosage guidelines, to help soothe itchy skin and promote overall skin health.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tinctures are good for itchy skin because it has anti-inflammatory properties. This means it helps reduce swelling and redness caused by skin irritation. The tincture also has antihistamine effects, which are similar to the medicine you take for allergies. When you have an allergic reaction, your body releases chemicals that make you itch. The antihistamines in Echinacea purpurea tincture can block these chemicals, giving you relief from itching. Additionally, the tincture is rich in antioxidants that help soothe and calm the skin, reducing the urge to scratch. Some people also believe that Echinacea purpurea tincture can help the body fight off infections that cause skin irritation, like fungal or bacterial infections. This may help your skin heal faster and feel better.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Plantago lanceolata tincture that helps with itchy skin, start by gathering a few handfuls of fresh or dried leaves of the Plantain plant. Choose a clean glass jar with a lid and fill it about 2/3 with the leaves. Next, pour vodka or a high-proof grain alcohol over the leaves, making sure they are completely covered. The ratio is usually 1 part Plantain leaves to 2-3 parts alcohol. Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two. This will allow the plant's active compounds to infuse into the alcohol. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids. Your Plantago lanceolata tincture is now ready to use, typically taken in small amounts of 10-30 drops under the tongue, 2-3 times a day.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Plantago lanceolata herbal tincture that helps with itchy skin, first, you need to dry the Plantago lanceolata leaves by tying them in small bunches and hanging them upside down in a warm, dry place. Once dry, you can use a coffee grinder or a mortar and pestle to finely chop the leaves into a powder. Next, mix one part of the dried leaf powder with three parts of vodka in a clean glass jar. The vodka helps to extract the healing properties of the Plantago lanceolata into the liquid. Secure the lid tightly and store the jar in a cool, dark place for about two weeks, shaking the jar gently every day. This allows the tincture to steep and the healing properties to be extracted into the liquid. After two weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Finally, transfer the tincture to a smaller dropper bottle and store it in the fridge to keep it fresh. You can take 20 to 30 drops of the tincture, three times a day, to help relieve itchy skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale tincture is made from the comfrey plant, which has been used for centuries to soothe skin problems. The active compounds in comfrey, like allantoin and rosmarinic acid, help to calm itchy skin and reduce inflammation. When applied topically, the tincture can penetrate deep into the skin, reducing redness and irritation. Comfrey's soothing effects are thought to be due to its ability to stimulate collagen production and promote skin healing, which can help to repair damaged skin and reduce the urge to scratch. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Plantago lanceolata tincture for itchy skin, start by gathering fresh or dried Plantago lanceolata leaves. Choose a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid and fill it with the Plantago lanceolata leaves, leaving about an inch of space at the top. Next, pour a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil over the leaves, making sure they are completely covered. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every few days. After the infusion time has passed, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. To make the tincture, combine the infused oil with a solvent such as vodka or glycerin in a ratio of 1:1 or 1:2 (solvent:infused oil). Shake the mixture well and store it in a cool, dark place. When you're ready to use the tincture, simply apply a few drops to the itchy area and gently massage it in. You can also add the tincture to a carrier oil or cream for easier application.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures can be a great natural remedy for itchy skin. One of the reasons it's essential to fix this issue is that it can be a sign of an underlying problem, such as eczema, allergies, or even skin conditions like psoriasis. If left untreated, these conditions can lead to more severe symptoms, like bleeding, scarring, and even infection.  Two herbs that have been used to soothe itchy skin are Calendula officinalis and Urtica dioica. Calendula, also known as marigold, is known for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. It can help calm the skin, reduce redness, and prevent infection. Urtica, or nettle leaf, is rich in antioxidants and has been shown to reduce inflammation and itching.  By using herbal tinctures, you can avoid the harsh chemicals found in commercial creams and ointments, which can further irritate the skin. Instead, you can create a natural solution that addresses the root cause of the itching, promoting healthy skin and reducing the risk of complications. This can improve your quality of life by allowing you to feel more comfortable in your own skin, reducing the stress and anxiety that comes with dealing with itchy skin, and giving you the confidence to participate in activities you enjoy.
supplementary_best: The best combination for itchy skin is Calendula officinalis and Urtica dioica tinctures. Calendula officinalis is a natural anti-inflammatory and soothing agent that can calm irritated skin. Urtica dioica, also known as nettle root, is high in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce itching and inflammation. When combined, they create a synergistic effect that can quickly soothe and calm itchy skin.  The Calendula officinalis helps to reduce redness and swelling, while Urtica dioica works to eliminate the underlying inflammation that causes itchiness. This combination is effective because it targets the root cause of the itchiness, rather than just masking the symptoms. The Urtica dioica also helps to lock in the benefits of the Calendula officinalis, making the combination more potent and longer-lasting.  Taking 20-30 drops of the combination tincture, 2-3 times a day, can provide quick relief from itchy skin. It's essential to dilute the tincture in water before applying it to the affected area, as undiluted tinctures can be too potent and cause further irritation.
supplementary_avoid: If you have itchy skin, it's a good idea to avoid herbal tinctures that can cause skin irritation or make itchiness worse. One of these herbs is Goldenseal (Hydrastis canadensis). Its active compound, berberine, can cause allergic reactions, including skin irritation and itching. Another herb to avoid is Elderberry (Sambucus nigra). Its berries, leaves, and stems contain anthraquinones, which can cause skin irritation, especially if you have sensitive skin. You should also be cautious with Dandelion (Taraxacum officinale) root, as it can cause skin reactions in some people. Burdock (Arctium lappa) root, while generally considered safe, may cause skin irritation in some individuals, particularly if taken in large quantities or for extended periods. Finally, avoid Comfrey (Cynara scolymus) root, as its compounds can cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in some people. If you're unsure about any herbal tincture, consult with a healthcare professional or a qualified herbalist before using it.
supplementary_other_remedies: Aloe vera gel is a natural remedy that can help with itchy skin. It has anti-inflammatory properties that calm and soothe irritated skin, reducing the itching and redness. Aloe vera gel works by reducing swelling and promoting healing, which can help to relieve itchy skin caused by sunburn, bug bites, or other skin irritations.  Oatmeal is another natural remedy that can provide relief from itchy skin. When added to bath water, oatmeal can help to reduce inflammation and itching by forming a protective barrier on the skin's surface. Oatmeal also has anti-irritant properties that can help to calm and soothe itchy skin, making it a popular ingredient in skin care products.  Cool compresses, such as a cold, wet cloth, can also help to relieve itchy skin. By reducing heat and inflammation, cool compresses can provide quick relief from itchy skin caused by sunburn, bug bites, or other skin irritations. When applied to the skin, cool compresses can help to reduce itching and promote healing, which can lead to faster recovery from itchy skin conditions.  Coconut oil is a natural moisturizer that can help to soothe itchy skin by locking in moisture and reducing inflammation. When applied topically to itchy skin, coconut oil can help to calm and soothe irritated skin, reducing the itching and redness associated with skin irritations. Coconut oil's anti-inflammatory properties can also help to promote healing, which can lead to faster recovery from itchy skin conditions.  Baking soda is a natural remedy that can help to relieve itchy skin by reducing inflammation and neutralizing skin pH. When mixed with water to form a paste, baking soda can be applied directly to itchy skin to help reduce itching and promote healing. Baking soda's anti-inflammatory properties can also help to calm and soothe irritated skin, making it a popular ingredient in skin care products.  These natural remedies can be used alone or in combination to help relieve itchy skin. For example, applying aloe vera gel or coconut oil to itchy skin can be followed by a cool compress or oatmeal bath to provide additional relief. By understanding the properties and uses of these natural remedies, individuals can find effective ways to manage itchy skin and promote healing.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with itchy skin often experience other health issues. Dry skin is common, and it can be caused by lack of moisture. Medicinal herbs like aloe vera can help. Aloe vera has moisturizing properties that soothe and hydrate the skin.  Frequent scratching can lead to skin infections, like eczema and dermatitis. These conditions can be painful and cause more itching. Herbs like chamomile can help reduce inflammation and calm the skin. Chamomile has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe irritated skin.  Itchy skin can also be a sign of an underlying condition, like allergies or digestive issues. For example, people with irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) may experience itchy skin due to food sensitivities. Herbs like turmeric can help reduce inflammation in the gut and skin. Turmeric contains curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the skin and gut.  Some people with itchy skin may experience anxiety and stress. Herbs like lavender can help calm the mind and body. Lavender has a calming effect that can reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.  People with itchy skin may also experience digestive issues, like bloating and gas. Herbs like peppermint can help soothe the digestive system. Peppermint has a calming effect on the digestive system, which can help reduce bloating and gas.  In addition, itchy skin can be a sign of a nutrient deficiency, like vitamin B7 (biotin). Herbs like alfalfa can help increase biotin levels in the body. Alfalfa is rich in biotin, which can help promote healthy skin and hair.  Herbs like oatmeal can also help soothe itchy skin. Oatmeal has anti-inflammatory properties that can calm irritated skin and reduce itching.
